post.hook: Dependency confusion, tag mutation, build hijacking — your pipeline is an attack surface.
post.body:
CI/CD pipelines hold secrets, build artifacts, and deployment credentials. A single compromised dependency or poisoned build step can cascade through your entire software supply chain. This package covers 7 supply chain attack techniques end-to-end.

What's inside the CI/CD Supply Chain Defense SOaC package:
→ 10 detection rules (Sigma, KQL, SPL, CrowdStrike, Wazuh)
→ 2 SOAR playbooks
→ 6 policy & runbook documents
→ 1 validated replay scenarios
→ MITRE ATT&CK: T1195.002, T1059.004, T1552.001, T1496, T1588.001, T1105, T1071.001

Every detection in this Elite-tier package has been validated against real attack telemetry in github_actions, gitlab_ci, linux, container_registry. The included SOAR playbooks integrate with your existing stack — no vendor lock-in, no proprietary formats. Detections ship as code. Playbooks ship as code. Policies ship as code.
post.cta: 💬 Comment “CICD” and I’ll DM you the full package.
post.full_text:
Dependency confusion, tag mutation, build hijacking — your pipeline is an attack surface.

CI/CD pipelines hold secrets, build artifacts, and deployment credentials. A single compromised dependency or poisoned build step can cascade through your entire software supply chain. This package covers 7 supply chain attack techniques end-to-end.

What's inside the CI/CD Supply Chain Defense SOaC package:
→ 10 detection rules (Sigma, KQL, SPL, CrowdStrike, Wazuh)
→ 2 SOAR playbooks
→ 6 policy & runbook documents
→ 1 validated replay scenarios
→ MITRE ATT&CK: T1195.002, T1059.004, T1552.001, T1496, T1588.001, T1105, T1071.001

Every detection in this Elite-tier package has been validated against real attack telemetry in github_actions, gitlab_ci, linux, container_registry. The included SOAR playbooks integrate with your existing stack — no vendor lock-in, no proprietary formats. Detections ship as code. Playbooks ship as code. Policies ship as code.

💬 Comment “CICD” and I’ll DM you the full package.

#SOaC #OpenCLAW #CyberSecurity #ThreatDetection #CICD #SupplyChainSecurity #PipelineSecurity #DependencyConfusion #GitHubActions #ContainerSecurity

engagement.first_comment:
🔗 Full package on GitHub: https://github.com/ge0mant1s/SOaC-Enterprise/tree/main/packages/013_ci_cd_supply_chain_defense

One detail not in the post: the 10 detection rules include cross-platform correlation logic — a single alert fires when activity matches across multiple telemetry sources, reducing false positives by focusing on attack chains rather than isolated events.
engagement.poll_question: Does your org scan CI artifacts and verify build provenance before deployment?
engagement.follow_up_day3: 48 hours in — people asked for this. Here's one thing the package catches that most SIEMs miss: build artifact hash mutation detection — catches tag poisoning before compromised packages reach production.
